ops.title: Substrate guard: one live present-handler per subject
ops.description:
The adversarial review (2026-07-20) REFUTED the claim that single-handler serialization is enforced: `store.RegisterAgent` keys on (subject_id, agent_id) with no uniqueness on agent_type, `teeToSubscribers` tees every subscriber, and board writes have no CAS — so a dispatch race (re-dispatch before the old child's stop lands, or a kill-9'd handler lingering as StatusRunning) puts two live handlers on one board, each teed the same events, producing interleaved/duplicate redrafts. Today the only guard is skill prose (TaskStop before re-dispatch).

Candidate mechanisms (cc-interact side): (a) a `SingletonSubscriber` config knob — registering a subscribing agent supersedes/closes prior running subscribers of the same (subject, agent_type); (b) tee only to the most-recently-registered subscriber per type; (c) surface a `agent.superseded` event so the old child's next drain tells it to exit. Also consider the related refuter finding: reduced state cannot distinguish owed-from-done for decision/choice/pack live reactions (no handled-marker) — a board-visible handled-marker (or reply-keyed markers) would make reconcile-on-start deterministic instead of heuristic.

Needs a cc-interact minor bump + cc-present config adoption.

ops.text: Two concurrently-registered present-handler agents on one subject cannot both receive teed events (test pins it)
A superseded handler learns it should exit via its mailbox or stop gate
cc-present adopts the guard in buildServer config and the skill prose drops the TaskStop-before-redispatch correctness caveat (keeping it as hygiene)
